summaries.113192: Comments identify two distinct causes: iOS cursor opacity animation runs continuously while focused (related to #108187 and #59327), and debug mode exaggerates the issue — release/profile mode should be used for performance measurement. A workaround was added exposing `cursorOpacityAnimates: false` on `TextField` to eliminate the CPU spike with minimal visual difference. Issue remains open with no fix landed; some commenters report crashes with Impeller that were redirected to separate issues.
summaries.72816: Root cause confirmed by a commenter's investigation: Android's `dispatchKeyEvent` in `FlutterView` emits paired down/up events on emulators, and this is Android emulator behavior — on real devices with a physical Bluetooth keyboard, key-repeat down events fire correctly and up fires only on release. The issue is emulator-specific, not a Flutter bug. A separate web-specific variant (key-up for held key when another key is pressed simultaneously) was noted as a distinct issue filed at #182775.
summaries.58244: Root cause identified as missing Visual Viewport API support in iOS 11–12 (the API landed in iOS 13). A commenter pinpointed the affected engine code in `dom_renderer.dart` and `window.dart`. Confirmed not reproducible on iOS 13+/14+/15+. The issue persists on old iOS versions through at least stable 3.19/3.20; no fix or PR was mentioned and the issue is stalled.
summaries.50163: Confirmed iOS-only (not Android) by early commenters; also occurs in Korean/other CJK input modes. Root cause is the engine calling `userUpdateTextEditingValue` twice for IME input. A `Future.delayed(10ms)` workaround was shared that defers `onChanged` until the controller text stabilizes. Still reproducible on stable 3.7 and 3.10; a commenter reports the underlying bug reappeared in flutter version 3.35.7 via issue #134507.
summaries.132373: Some reviewers initially could not reproduce; others consistently reproduced on Flutter 3.10.x–3.16.x on web (Chrome and Edge on both macOS and Windows). The flicker occurs specifically when `fillColor` and `hoverColor` are set to the same color — setting `hoverColor: Colors.transparent` stops the flicker and is the accepted workaround. A related symptom (gray background in helper/error text area) was noted even with the workaround applied. Still reproducible on 3.32.5 with no fix landed.
summaries.130654: Root cause narrowed by the reporter: the bug occurs only with the canvaskit renderer + `StrutStyle()` + `useMaterial3: true`; the html renderer and no-StrutStyle cases are unaffected. The IME issue specifically affects multi-keystroke Japanese input (e.g., 'ka' = k+a) as the first character, on both Windows and macOS. Console errors about `HardwareKeyboard` key-up/down mismatches were noted but considered a separate issue. Confirmed still occurring on stable 3.16.5; assignee was removed due to inactivity.
summaries.59519: Confirmed reproducible on web, macOS desktop, Android emulator, and iOS simulator. The core issue is that Tab key traversal ignores custom `FocusNode` ordering set via `onEditingComplete`, jumping to the next widget in tree order instead. A comment noted `onEditingComplete` and `onSubmitted` also do not receive Tab key events. No fix or PR was proposed; issue is stalled with triaged labels only.
summaries.76798: Confirmed reproducible on physical iOS devices (not simulator). A commenter identified the relevant engine line in `FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm`. The log message was also reproduced with a native Swift app (no Flutter), suggesting it originates from the iOS system's snapshotting mechanism. One commenter confirmed it also appears when using VoiceOver. No fix has been proposed; the issue has been re-routed to the iOS embedding team.
summaries.67875: Root cause explained: Flutter draws selection handles within its own window and cannot render over the system keyboard, which is a separate system-level app. The disappearing-handle-on-drag bug is a separate sub-issue. No fix or PR was proposed for either sub-issue; the issue is stalled as a known architectural limitation, confirmed reproducible on stable 3.16 and master 3.18.
summaries.30725: Discussion extensively covers three architectural approaches for macOS: (1) let `NSKeybindingManager` map keys to intents and send those to the framework, (2) track key sequences that trigger `doCommandBySelector:`, or (3) preprocess key events before sending to `NSTextInputContext`. Approach 1 was favored as a starting point. Framework-side shortcuts via `Shortcuts` already work for common combos but do not respect user-customized macOS key bindings. The issue blocks IME accent-menu support (#78061) and is also relevant to physical keyboards on iPad. Still open; `_macShortcuts` removal was planned but not confirmed done.
summaries.22171: Comments confirm the behavior is intentional by design (the framework explicitly drops selection on `.text =` assignment) but considered a fidelity gap. The workaround — read the selection before and restore it after — causes a visible cursor flicker on Android. Confirmed reproducible on iOS and Android through stable 3.13 and master 3.17; no fix or PR has been proposed.
summaries.155330: Root cause identified by @LongCatIsLooong: fonts with a non-zero typo line gap cause `getFullHeightForCaret` to return a shorter height for trailing spaces; the fix is to use `getRectsForRange` instead but test fonts all use 0 line gap making it hard to test. Linked to #154039 as a possible regression source. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.24.3 through 3.29.3 and master 3.32; assignee removed due to inactivity.
summaries.156380: Root cause identified by a commenter: Tab is pressed while a Korean character is still in the composing state (underlined), which causes focus to skip the next field. Pressing the right arrow key first to finalize composition, then Tab, moves focus correctly. The issue is specific to macOS Chrome (not Safari, not Windows Chrome). Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.24–3.32.4; no fix or PR proposed.
summaries.154072: Root cause identified from a macOS crash stack trace: `fml::Utf16ToUtf8` aborts when converting the text after emoji insertion because the byte offset used to set the selection (`characterToInsert.length` in UTF-16 code units) is invalid for the engine's internal model. Confirmed on macOS and Linux desktop (not Android/iOS). A Windows-specific freeze variant (infinite message loop after `requestFocus`) was reported separately. Affects macOS/Windows/Linux; issue is in `FlutterTextInputPlugin` in the engine. No PR proposed.
summaries.35706: The issue was relabeled to track the need for a debug-mode overflow warning in `InputDecorator`. A prior fix (#63639) was reverted (#64573), and a follow-up attempt (#110966) was closed without merging. The latest analysis by @LongCatIsLooong proposes either changing the layout algorithm to prioritize text over content padding in single-line fields, or adding an opt-in `debugInputDecoratorOverflow` flag. Assignee was removed due to inactivity; no fix landed.
summaries.86354: Comments confirm the issue: `TextEditingController.clear()` directly mutates the value without going through `EditableText`'s formatter pipeline, so `formatEditUpdate` is never called. No workaround beyond calling a cleanup method on the formatter manually was suggested. Confirmed reproducible on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32; no fix or PR proposed.
summaries.89780: Root cause is Android OS behavior: when the keyboard appears it forces system UI overlays back, and Android does not restore the previous UI state until 1 second after keyboard dismissal. The white bar is the system navigation area reappearing. Confirmed reproducible on Android API 29–30 emulators; not reproducible on API 33+. The documentation describes the expected behavior but the white space instead of the proper UI is the gap. No fix beyond upgrade recommendations was provided.
summaries.142040: Root cause identified: `SelectableText`/`EditableText` uses an offscreen `<input>` HTML element as the web embedder backing, and focus-related interactions with this element prevent focus from leaving the Flutter iframe. `SelectionArea` (which does not use `EditableText`) was confirmed as a workaround by @justinmc. The behavior changed on master (original copy-on-drag now works) but a new regression was introduced where clicking into the iframe prevents focus from ever returning to the parent page. DevTools filed follow-up issues (#8929, #8939) and is migrating from `SelectableText` to `SelectionArea`. Assignee removed due to inactivity.
